PHASE:        Phase 8 Batch 2 Item 4 SS-2 SQUASH-MERGED — Patrols Export Route Handler merged to main as commit 52869ae. Push to origin/main + feat/exports-patrols branch deletion pending user confirmation (matches the SS-1 final-ship sequence in commits 3ed3382 → 1c783cd). Second per-entity export endpoint live: /api/exports/patrols GET handler with manual auth, upload rate-limit tier, 10001-take overflow → HTTP 413, sha256 filterHash, CSV/PDF branches, DATA_EXPORT audit. Export buttons on /patrols page (replaced the stub). Pattern from SS-1 copied verbatim — only the entity, the filter shape (state + patrolType vs state + priority), and the 10 columns changed. SS-3 (Alert Rules) and SS-4 (Notifications + Alert History combined) remain. Prior same-day: SS-1 Events Export (commits 0171309 + 3ed3382 + 1c783cd shipped to origin/main), SS-0 Export Foundation (commit 4caa449), Alert History Log (commit 7da0cab), Notification.patrolId FK (commit 895fa84).

LAST_DONE:    Phase 8 Batch 2 Item 4 SS-2 — Patrols Export feature work committed (or ready to commit) on feat/exports-patrols branch.
              Files added (apps/web/src/app/api/exports/patrols/):
              - `route.ts`: GET Route Handler with `// Non-tRPC: manual auth required` header (security.md L11). Flow: requireRouteAuth() → rateLimiters.upload.check(userId) (20/min) → patrolListFilters.safeParse(state+patrolType from query) → prisma.tenant.findUniqueOrThrow for slug+name → prisma.patrol.findMany with `take: 10001` for overflow detection → HTTP 413 JSON if length > 10000 → flatten to typed PatrolRow (10 columns: id, serialNumber, title, patrolType, state, boatName, startTime ISO, endTime ISO, totalDistanceKm string, createdAt ISO) → sha256 filterHash from sorted-keys JSON.stringify(filters) → branch on format=csv|pdf → toCsv() or renderExportPdf() → writeExportAudit() AFTER success with filterHash + rowCount → NextResponse with Content-Type/Content-Disposition/Cache-Control: no-store. PDF Buffer wrapped in Uint8Array for NextResponse. Pattern identical to SS-1 events handler.
              - `__tests__/route.test.ts`: 6 tests — CSV byte-level BOM (the SS-1 🟡 lesson carried forward; assertions use `new Uint8Array(await res.arrayBuffer())` from the start) + tenant-scoped findMany, PDF content-type, 401 missing session, 413 when findMany returns 10001 rows + no audit row written, DATA_EXPORT audit shape with 64-char-hex filterHash + changesJson={format,rowCount}, state+patrolType filter propagation into prisma.where. Mocks @/server/auth, @marine-guardian/db (patrol/tenant/auditLog), @react-pdf/renderer's renderToBuffer (spread retains the React components needed by export-pdf.tsx at module-load).

              Files modified:
              - `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/patrol.ts`: extracted `patrolListFilters = z.object({state, patrolType})` as a new top-level export. `patrolRouter.list` now uses `patrolListFilters.extend({cursor, limit})` for its input. Mirrors the SS-1 eventListFilters extract — single source of truth for the Route Handler to reuse. SS-3/SS-4 routers will follow the same pattern (alertRuleListFilters, notificationListFilters).
              - `apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/patrols/page.tsx`: replaced the 1-paragraph stub with a flex header holding the title on the left and a 2-button group on the right (Export CSV + Export PDF). Both buttons rendered as `<Button asChild variant="outline" size="sm">` wrapping `<a href={buildExportUrl("patrols", {}, format)} download>`. Same shape as the events page (no Link — Next.js Link doesn't honor `download`).
              - `docs/CHANGELOG_AI.md`: new SS-2 entry prepended above SS-1.
              - `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_MAP.md`: Last updated line refreshed; SS-2 item flipped from `[ ]` to `[x]` with details; Next Step section rewritten to reflect SS-3 + SS-4 as the only remaining export sub-sessions; "Shipped since" list adds the SS-2 entry alongside the SS-1 final-ship marker.

              Schema/migrations: none — SS-2 is application code only.

              Acceptance gate (all GREEN, no fixups):
              - typecheck 6/6 packages clean
              - lint 5/5 packages clean (no new lint errors — the SS-1 fixes for template-literal numeric coercion and the partial() pattern were already in place via the test fixture conventions)
              - tests: 66/66 in apps/web (was 60 — added 6 patrols route handler tests) + 7/7 in packages/jobs (cached, unchanged) = 73 total
              - `pnpm --filter @marine-guardian/web build` — succeeds. Next.js registers `/api/exports/patrols` as ƒ Dynamic Route alongside `/api/exports/events`.
              - TDD order intact: wrote test file first → verified RED (module-not-found on ../route) → implemented → verified 6/6 GREEN on first run (no fixups needed — SS-1's BOM-via-arrayBuffer 🟡 lesson and the SS-1 lint fixes carried forward verbatim into the new test file).

              EXECUTION NOTES:
              Per the documented hook-injection thrashing lesson, stayed Opus-direct throughout. Hook injection still firing this session (vercel-plugin "next-forge" / "vercel-functions" / "next-cache-components" / "nextjs" suggestions on Read+Write; context-mode observation pointers; claude-mem timeline pointers; PostToolUse-validate suggestion on the new route file for "observability instrumentation"). Opus 4.7 absorbs them cleanly. Token usage well within the 60K SAFE feedback-memory threshold. No plan deviations of substance — SS-2 followed the SS-1 pattern exactly. One minor column-set choice: dropped `totalHours` from the export to match the SS-1 row count of 10 columns; kept `totalDistanceKm` + the start/end timestamps which allow downstream consumers to derive hours if needed.

              Plan deviations (1, minor): Plan said "mirror whatever the patrolRouter.list currently validates" — the actual schema is only state+patrolType (plus pagination), so the Route Handler exposes those two filters and no others. Same single-source-of-truth principle as SS-1: code wins over the plan-file's wishlist.
